fixed_basis_theorem.alpha_independence: Delta_alpha is independent of s because the only s-dependent row is replaced by the objective row.
fixed_basis_theorem.epsilon_affinity: Delta_alpha has degree at most one in epsilon because epsilon occurs only in the single scale column.
fixed_basis_theorem.no_internal_sign_flip: At fixed epsilon with nonzero Delta_alpha, the alpha multiplier cannot change sign on a connected region where the active basis determinant is nonzero.

family_summary.interpretation: The numerator orientation is highly robust inside the A67 arithmetic-grid family, but the basis determinant orientation is phase dependent.

counterexample.conclusion: The same contact order and active-band signs can produce opposite Cramer-numerator signs. Order-only total positivity is insufficient for the coupled dual-envelope matrix.

next_target: Arithmetic-grid q-Vandermonde and Schur-positive factorization of the Cramer numerator.
